mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling from a restrictive level, the discount-rate channel dominates and equities reprice upward over the next month as expected policy rates decline faster than earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above its recent trend raises real-rate pressure and policy-tightening expectations, which compresses equity multiples and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.2%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-crossing months are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal's sign flips positive in a sustained inflation-acceleration regime.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE together with falling PAYEMS identifies labor-market deterioration that weakens expected consumption and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if joint-trigger episodes do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if PAYEMS declines without a concurrent UNRATE rise fail to carry the same effect.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output and softer future corporate revenues, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in a majority of months when INDPRO falls for two consecutive readings, or if the signal's rolling 3-year IC is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and near-term revenue momentum, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS trend months do not produce above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears once the series is stable above its 12-month average.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and GDPC1 both weaken, the housing channel and aggregate demand channel confirm a growth slowdown that should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month average while GDPC1 is negative on the latest quarterly print
failure_condition: Reject if joint HOUST-GDPC1 weakness is not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if either series alone fully explains the effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary or post-shock recovery phase, a high UNRATE level combined with rising FEDFUNDS signals policy restraint into labor slack, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with elevated UNRATE and rising FEDFUNDS do not underperform the unconditional 20-day return distribution, or if the sign is not consistently negative across similar post-shock episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is still below inflation, real rates stay negative and equities should rally over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate continued accommodation.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is at least 1.0 percentage point below the 12-month CPIAUCSL trend rate
failure_condition: Reject if low-real-rate episodes do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the effect disappears once inflation and policy are no longer jointly misaligned.
